title: AWS Production Security Group Change - CR-2026-0892
ai_executive_summary: This configuration change introduces multiple critical security vulnerabilities. SSH access has been opened to the entire internet (0.0.0.0/0), RDP port 3389 has been added without restriction, the database security group CIDR has been expanded from /24 to /8 exposing it to a significantly larger network, and CloudTrail and VPC Flow Logs have been disabled — removing all security monitoring capability. This change should be REJECTED immediately.
ai_recommendations: REJECT: Do not approve this change. It introduces at minimum 5 critical security violations.
Restrict SSH (port 22) to the bastion host IP or VPN CIDR range only (e.g., 10.10.1.0/24)
Remove the RDP (3389) inbound rule entirely — use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager instead
Revert the database security group CIDR from 10.0.0.0/8 back to 10.0.0.0/24
Re-enable CloudTrail and VPC Flow Logs immediately — their absence is a compliance violation
Review who submitted this change request and under what change management process

diff_changes.ai_explanation: SSH port 22 is now accessible from the entire internet. This is the highest priority security violation in this change set. Any attacker can attempt brute-force or exploit known SSH vulnerabilities.
RDP port 3389 has been opened to the internet. RDP is a frequent target for ransomware attacks and brute-force credential stuffing. This rule must be removed.
Database CIDR expanded from /24 (256 hosts) to /8 (16 million hosts). This exposes the PostgreSQL port to a vastly larger portion of the internal network, violating the principle of least privilege.
CloudTrail has been disabled. This removes the audit trail for all AWS API calls and is a violation of CIS AWS Benchmark 2.1, NIST AU-2, and PCI-DSS Requirement 10.
VPC Flow Logs have been disabled. Network traffic can no longer be audited. This is required by NIST 800-53 SI-4 and PCI-DSS Requirement 10.6.

compliance_findings.detail: Port 22 is open to 0.0.0.0/0 — violates CIS AWS Benchmark 4.1
Unrestricted internet access to management ports violates least privilege
Audit logging (CloudTrail + VPC Flow Logs) has been disabled
Ports 22 and 3389 open to all internet traffic

compliance_findings.control_name: Log all access to audit trails

compliance_findings.detail: Unrestricted access to SSH and RDP from internet
